summary: End-to-end testing of Agenda Italserrande PWA. Backend: 12/12 pytest tests pass (auth gates, clients/expenses/advances CRUD, monthly summary with aggregation, and per-user data isolation across two real Firebase users). Frontend: full flow works after fixing a critical bug — Spese.jsx had a variable shadowing issue (`it` loop variable shadowed `it` Italian locale from date-fns/locale), causing 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading preprocessor)' runtime crash whenever any expense was rendered. Fixed by renaming the loop variable to `exp`. After fix, register → login → add client (with payment method) → edit client → add advance → navigate to Spese → add/edit expense → Riepilogo (totals reflect data, month nav works) → Profilo (shows email) → logout → re-login (data persists) all work cleanly with no JS errors.

ui_bugs.issue: [FIXED by testing agent] Variable name `it` (loop variable) shadowed imported `it` Italian locale from date-fns/locale. Caused TypeError 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading preprocessor)' at format() and an 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay whenever the Spese page rendered any expense row. Renamed loop var to `exp`.

action_items: Review the variable-shadowing fix in /app/frontend/src/pages/Spese.jsx (loop variable `it` → `exp`); verify no other files do `(it) =>` while also importing the date-fns Italian locale `it`.
critical_code_review_comments: Variable shadowing risk: any file that imports `it` from 'date-fns/locale' must avoid using `it` as a loop/iteration variable. Consider renaming the import alias (e.g. `import { it as itLocale } from 'date-fns/locale'`) project-wide to make this class of bug impossible.
Backend `/api/me` returns 403 without token (FastAPI HTTPBearer default) instead of 401. Tests accept both — works as-is, but 401 is more idiomatic for missing credentials.

Backend uses `{$regex: ^YYYY-MM}` for month filtering on string dates. OK for current scale, but no anchored escape — fine because input is constrained to ISO dates from FE; still consider a safer prefix/range query.
`ExpenseSource` Literal includes only `contanti` and `conto_aziendale`; if user later wants more sources, this is a breaking enum change.
test_credentials: Firebase users created dynamically per run via Firebase REST signUp endpoint (key from REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY). Format: testuser+<timestamp><suffix>@italserrande.test / TestPassword123!
seed_data_creation: Tests create their own ephemeral data (TEST_ prefix) and clean up via DELETE in teardown. Two distinct Firebase users used for isolation tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable as regression. It uses Firebase REST signUp/signIn (no admin SDK on test side) so it works as long as the Firebase project keeps Email/Password enabled. Two-user data isolation test included. Test users accumulate in Firebase Auth — purge periodically. For frontend, on desktop viewport the sidebar nav has data-testid='nav-spese'/etc visible; on mobile the bottom nav uses the same testids — both are present in the DOM, so use .first or filter by visibility in Playwright.
rca of the issue: Spese.jsx imported the Italian locale as `import { it } from 'date-fns/locale'` and then iterated expenses with `items.map((it) => ...)`. Inside the JSX, `format(parseISO(it.date), 'd MMM yyyy', { locale: it })` resolved `it` to the loop variable (the expense object) due to lexical scoping in arrow fn, not the locale. date-fns then tried to read `it.localize.preprocessor` on an Expense object → TypeError. Fix: renamed loop var to `exp`. Verified no JS errors after fix and full e2e flow now passes.
